data.narrative:
Phaidra is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Jim Gao (ex-Google DeepMind industrial AI lead), Vedavyas Panneershelvam (AlphaGo engineer), and Katherine Hoffman (operations, ex-Trane Technologies). The valuation analysis draws on two methodologies triangulated against a universe of 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir).

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement to Phaidra's $120M total raised, yielding $228M. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company comp set—including Physical Intelligence ($5.6B, Series B), Reflection AI ($8.0B, Series A), Together AI ($3.3B, Series A), Skild AI ($14.0B, Series C), and Figure AI ($39.0B, Series C)—produces a median valuation of $6.8B, with a range of $3.2B to $39.0B reflecting substantial stage and capability dispersion.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $194M (low), $3.5B (median), and $7.8B (high). The wide range reflects material uncertainty around Phaidra's commercial traction relative to peers, execution risk in capital-intensive frontier robotics and AI, and the inherent volatility of private comparables at this stage. Founder pedigree and technical depth support the upper end; revenue visibility and path-to-scale remain key valuation sensitivities.
